message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp ~87 reviews on the restaurant listing (parent Surf Club venue ~321); OpenTable listing live; Tripadvisor listing (under former name 'Navigator') shows only 1 review — overall low to moderate review volume for a high-profile opening
- Sentiment: mostly positive, with mixed service-execution notes
- Longevity: Restaurant concept opened with the Surf Club venue in 2024; rebranded to 'Navigator' November 2024 under Michael Hung; reverted to 'The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club' after Hung's departure (per search snippet dated Jan 2026) [UNVERIFIED on exact departure date and reversion timing]. Current artisanal menu credited to 'Chef Mondragon' [UNVERIFIED full name].
- Credentials: ["Cited by Locale Magazine as one of the most consequential new Palm Springs openings","Featured in Bon Traveler's Best Restaurants in Palm Springs","Chef Michael Hung's prior properties (Colony Club, SO.PA at L'Horizon) earned Michelin Guide listings — credential attaches to Hung, not directly to this restaurant"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for 'Palm Springs Surf Club restaurant', 'best new Palm Springs restaurants 2025', and 'surf-inspired Palm Springs'. Does NOT consistently appear in 'best restaurants Coachella Valley', 'fine dining Palm Desert' (wrong city/corridor), or generic 'date night Palm Springs' top-of-list — those are still dominated by Copley's, Workshop, The Purple Room, Mr. Lyons, Le Vallauris.
- Site structured data: Own site (palmspringssurfclub.com/the-restaurant/) carries clean address, phone, hours, Resy/OpenTable reservation hooks, and segmented Lunch/Dinner/Brunch menus, but no visible schema.org Restaurant JSON-LD detected in fetched content. Menus are linked as PDFs (e.g., Rest-HH-Menu.pdf) — agent-readable but not structured. Tripadvisor listing is unclaimed.
- Visibility gap score: 7
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Chef Michael Hung and Navigator at the Palm Springs Surf Club:

## Chef Michael Hung

Michael Hung is the Executive Chef of Navigator, and was formerly a chef at Faith & Flower and The Colony Club. His resume includes prestigious roles at New York's Daniel and Aquavit, as well as in San Francisco at Jardinière (under Chef Traci Des Jardins) and La Folie (with Chef Roland Passot), both of which received Michelin recognition.

## Navigator Restaurant

Navigator, the flagship restaurant of the Palm Springs Surf Club, officially opened on Friday, November 15, 2024. The restaurant blends modern American cuisine with global surf-inspired flavors.

Michael Hung serves as both the Executive Chef and Director of Food & Beverage at this surfing and dining hotspot. Navigator's menu highlights modern American cuisine infused with influences from renowned surf locales, including flavors inspired by Portugal, Morocco, Mexico, Indonesia, and Hawaii.

With a backdrop of San Jacinto Mountain views and the rhythmic pulse of the Surf Club's wave pool, Navigator redefines desert dining. Navigator is open to the public—no Surf Club membership is required.

**Note:** According to one source, Chef Michael Hung has since left the Palm Springs Surf Club, though the exact timing of his departure is not specified in these search results.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Palm Springs Surf Club restaurant rebrand and Chef Michael Hung's departure:

## Restaurant Rebrand to Navigator

The Palm Springs Surf Club rebranded its restaurant as Navigator, and Navigator officially opened on Friday, November 15, 2024.

## Chef Michael Hung and His Role

Executive Chef Michael Hung is an acclaimed culinary talent known for his work in high-end kitchens across the U.S., with prestigious roles at New York's Daniel and Aquavit, as well as in San Francisco at Jardinière and La Folie, both of which received Michelin recognition. He later served as Executive Chef for Steve Hermann Hotels, where he revitalized menus at The Colony Club and SO.PA, both of which earned Michelin Guide recognition.

## Hung's Departure in 2025

According to a more recent source from January 2026, Chef Michael Hung has left the Palm Springs Surf Club. However, the search results don't provide specific details about the exact timing or reasons for his departure in 2025.

## Current Status (as of 2025)

It's noteworthy that as of January 2025, when asked if the main restaurant is called Amala or Navigator, the Palm Springs Surf Club stated that the main restaurant on their property is The Restaurant at the Palm Springs Surf Club, suggesting the name may have reverted or changed from Navigator.

Based on the search results, I found information about Bon Traveler's coverage of restaurants in Palm Springs and details about the Palm Springs Surf Club restaurant.

Bon Traveler provides a guide to the 20 best restaurants in Palm Springs, featuring everything from hip cafes to chic bars for Palm Springs dining.

Regarding the Palm Springs Surf Club specifically, the main restaurant on the property is The Restaurant at the Palm Springs Surf Club, with a more casual, smaller restaurant called Drifters located by the surf pool. The chefs prepare fresh, seasonal ingredients with a menu that reflects the essence of the Golden State, celebrating sun-drenched fields, coastal waters and valley gardens, embracing a philosophy of fresh, vibrant and locally sourced products.

There is no fee to enter the Restaurant at the Palm Springs Surf Club, and while a reservation is strongly suggested but not required, you can book through their OpenTable page.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Navigator at the Palm Springs Surf Club:

## Navigator Restaurant Opening

The Palm Springs Surf Club has opened its newest restaurant Navigator. The restaurant, which opens to the public on Friday, offers a stylish indoor dining area, a vibrant bar, and an outdoor patio with breathtaking views of the club's surf pool and the San Jacinto Mountains.

## Cuisine & Chef

The restaurant boasts "California cuisine inspired by renowned surf beaches throughout the world." At the helm of Navigator is Executive Chef Michael Hung, an acclaimed culinary talent known for his work in high-end kitchens across the U.S. In Los Angeles, Chef Hung earned accolades for his work at Faith & Flower and Viviane at the Avalon Hotel. He later brought his expertise to Palm Springs, serving as Executive Chef for Steve Hermann Hotels, where he revitalized menus at The Colony Club and SO.PA, both of which earned Michelin Guide recognition.

## Menu & Design

At Navigator, Chef Hung brings his global culinary perspective to an elevated dining experience, blending modern American cuisine with influences from iconic surf destinations such as Portugal, Morocco, Mexico, Indonesia, and Hawaii. The design of Navigator is inspired by the oceanic traditions of South Pacific navigators, who used the stars, currents, and wildlife to guide their way across vast seas. The restaurant's décor reflects this maritime influence, with custom teakwood furniture, woven textile ceiling treatments, and a hand-carved outrigger canoe as a focal point in the dining room.

## Hours & Access

Navigator will be open for dinner seven days a week from 5:00 pm to 9:00 pm. Navigator is open to the public—no Surf Club membership required.

Colony Club is a MICHELIN restaurant located in Palm Springs. It is situated at The Colony Palms Hotel and Bungalows, 572 N. Indian Canyon Dr., Palm Springs, CA, 92262.

The restaurant is described as having significant historical charm. This Moroccan-style oasis at the plush Colony Palms Hotel has history and charm to spare, having once been the stomping ground of stars like Clark Gable and Humphrey Bogart.

In terms of the dining experience, the menu is laid-back, from snacks like edamame hummus, to heftier fare like a grilled pork chop with cheesy polenta, with appetizers perhaps like the scallop aguachile, before moving on to mains like chicken pot pie.

content.text: Now I have a clearer picture. Original name was Amala (Jan 2024), rebranded to Navigator (Nov 2024 under Hung), then reverted to "The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club" — NOT back to Amala. The brief's claim that "concept opened with the Surf Club venue in 2024" is correct, but the original name was Amala (the brief omits this). The "Chef Mondragon" credit is tied to Amala-era, and may or may not still be active.

confirms: Restaurant concept opened with the Surf Club venue in 2024 (January 1, 2024) — confirmed via press releases
Rebranded to 'Navigator' under Michael Hung — opened November 15, 2024 (confirmed via Daily Ovation, PSTribune, NBC Palm Springs, CV Independent)

Reverted to 'The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club' after Hung's departure — confirmed via official site FAQ explicitly stating 'Neither [Amala nor Navigator]. The main restaurant on our property is The Restaurant at the Palm Springs Surf Club'
Tripadvisor listing under former 'Navigator' name shows only 1 review (4-star, posted March 2026) — directly confirmed

Chef Michael Hung's prior properties Colony Club and SO.PA at L'Horizon earned Michelin Guide listings — Colony Club confirmed in guide.michelin.com; SO.PA confirmed as Michelin-listed (currently led by Chef Giacomo Pettinari, the Coachella Valley's only Michelin-starred chef)
Bon Traveler maintains a '20 Best Restaurants in Palm Springs' list (the article cited exists)
contradicts: CLAIM: 'Featured in Bon Traveler's Best Restaurants in Palm Springs' — REFUTED. The Bon Traveler 20 Best Restaurants in Palm Springs article does NOT include The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club, Navigator, or any Surf Club venue. The 20 listed are: Workshop Kitchen + Bar, Rooster and the Pig, 4 Saints, Birba, King's Highway, Counter Reformation, Azucar, Sandfish, The Barn Kitchen, T&T Innovation Kitchen, Del Rey, Wilma & Frieda, The Pink Cabana, Blackbook, El Jefe, Kreem, Elmer's, Chef Tanya's Kitchen, Farm, Bar Cecil.

CLAIM: 'Cited by Locale Magazine as one of the most consequential new Palm Springs openings' — MISATTRIBUTED. The Locale Magazine 'top 12' feature was for AMALA Restaurant & Bar (the Jan 2024 original name of the Surf Club restaurant), per the venue's own Facebook post. While Amala was the predecessor concept, conflating this credential with the current 'The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club' is imprecise — and Locale's framing was 'top 12' (likely a year-end list), not 'most consequential new openings.'

CLAIM: 'Current artisanal menu credited to Chef Mondragon' — LIKELY MISATTRIBUTED. 'Chef Mondragon' is consistently tied to the AMALA era (pre-Navigator rebrand) in venue marketing and the canva.site Amala menu. The current 'The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club' official page does NOT name any chef. No independent source confirms Mondragon is the current chef of the post-Hung incarnation.
flagged_as_unverified: Exact date of Michael Hung's departure from the Palm Springs Surf Club — multiple sources reference it happened but none give a specific date in 2025

Exact reversion timing from 'Navigator' back to 'The Restaurant at The Palm Springs Surf Club' — Tripadvisor still uses 'Navigator' as the listing name as of March 2026, while the official site uses 'The Restaurant'
Chef Mondragon's full name — only surfaced as a LinkedIn entry for a 'Rodrigo Munoz' (not Mondragon) associated with Amala; Mondragon's first name not independently verifiable
Yelp review count of ~87 — appeared in search snippet but Yelp page returned 403 and could not be directly verified
OpenTable listing being live and parent Surf Club Yelp ~321 reviews — not directly verified in this lens

Whether Hung personally 'earned' the Michelin Guide listings for Colony Club and SO.PA, or whether the listings predate/postdate his tenure — Michelin recognition for SO.PA is currently credited to Giacomo Pettinari
Sentiment characterization ('mostly positive, mixed service-execution notes') — Tripadvisor's 1 review does note service critiques, but this is not statistically meaningful
input.notes: The credentials section of this assessment has material problems. The Bon Traveler citation is flatly wrong — the Surf Club restaurant is not in their 20 Best list. The Locale Magazine citation is misattributed: it honored Amala (the Jan 2024 original concept), not the current rebranded restaurant. The 'Chef Mondragon' attribution to the current menu is also Amala-era and not corroborated by the official site, which is silent on chef leadership. The history timeline is more defensible but incomplete: the brief skips the Amala→Navigator→Restaurant chronology, calling the initial concept simply 'the restaurant concept' when it had a distinct name (Amala). Michelin credentials for Colony Club are confirmed; SO.PA's current Michelin recognition is tied to Pettinari, not Hung, weakening the 'Hung earned them' framing. The restaurant has a real but thin credential footprint — most concrete press is about the November 2024 Navigator launch, which has since been undone. Recommend rewriting the credentials list to (a) remove the Bon Traveler claim, (b) clarify that the Locale Magazine feature was for the predecessor Amala concept, (c) drop or qualify the Mondragon attribution, and (d) note that the venue is on its third restaurant identity in under two years, which is itself a notable instability signal for an 'anchor-reference' tier classification.
